title: How to see if there is a file attached to a specific task in the task overview?
excerpt:
The task overview shows an icon if there is a comment, but you can’t see if a file is attached to a task without opening the individual task and opening the “Files” tab. 
It would give an easier overview if one could …

posts.author: Sbi (Søren Bilde)
posts.cookedHtml:
image803×199 11.9 KB

The task overview shows an icon if there is a comment, but you can’t see if a file is attached to a task without opening the individual task and opening the “Files” tab.
It would give an easier overview if one could make it visible on the overview if there was a file attached to a task.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Sbi
Welcome to the community. If you are using a file column there is no need to open the item updates.

posts.author: Sbi (Søren Bilde)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Basdebruin, thanks for the quick reply, but we would rather avoid making more columns

So we just wish that it was visually easy to see that there is an attachment on the individual task, a bit like one can on mails in Outlook and other mail clients.

posts.author: rickwhaas (Rick)
posts.cookedHtml:
We have this same issue and would like to see this icon added. Something as simple as a paperclip icon next to the comment icon would suffice.

We do have a “files” tab which shows files that were added from a form. But if a customer (guest) uploads additional files inside an update it does not show up in the files column which is causing issues with missing information.

posts.author: system (system)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.
